business.headline: Crude oil crosses $100 for first time in two months as US-Iran conflict intensifiesAt the time of writing, Brent Crude, the global oil benchmark, was trading at $100.14 a barrel, up 6.45%.

Infosys to roll out salary hikes from October, hire 20,000 freshersThe IT major said the salary hike cycle will be implemented in three parts. While the company did not share details of the percentage increase, it confirmed that the rollout will begin in October and continue into January for different employee groups.

Infosys posts 12% jump in Q1 profit, trims upper end of FY27 growth outlookThe IT major posted a consolidated net profit of Rs 7,769 crore for the quarter ended June 30, 2026, compared with Rs 6,921 crore in the same period last year. Revenue from operations rose 14% to Rs 48,211 crore, up from Rs 42,279 crore a year ago.

Infosys appoints Ashiss Kumar Dash as CEO-designateAccording to the company, Dash will begin his role as CEO-designate with immediate effect. The Board has also approved its intention to appoint him as the Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director from April 1, 2027.

Crude oil nears $100: Why rising price is bad news for IndiaAt the time of writing, Brent crude was trading at $98.28 per barrel, up 4.48%, reflecting growing concerns over the geopolitical situation in the Middle East.

Retaining users is really hard: Nithin Kamath on broking business' biggest challengeKamath said one of the biggest hurdles for stockbrokers is user retention. While many people open trading accounts, not all continue investing or trading for years.

Sensex, Nifty extend losing streak to 4th day as Brent crude nears $100The BSE Sensex fell 363.66 points, or 0.47%, to close at 76,391.39, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 126.65 points, or 0.53%, to settle at 23,869.60. This marked Dalal Street's longest losing streak in seven weeks.

How FIFA turned the football World Cup into a $15 billion money machineFIFA used the expanded World Cup, higher ticket prices and new sales channels to drive record revenue. The tournament showed how nearly every part of football's biggest event is now being monetised.

JPMorgan to add 1,000 tech jobs across India GCCs despite AI-driven workforce changesThe new hiring will be aimed at technology specialists with expertise in cloud architecture, cybersecurity and AI data pipelines.

Don't click 'submit' yet! These 3 last-minute ITR checks are a mustFiling your ITR before the deadline is important, but filing it correctly is even more important. These three last-minute checks can help you avoid costly errors.

Physics Wallah founder Alakh Pandey joins Jantar Mantar protestAlakh Pandey joined the CJP protest at Jantar Mantar and urged the government to speak with students. His intervention came after criticism over his silence and widened the row beyond NEET to education and recruitment.

Uber layoffs: Company cuts 10% of customer service jobs as AI adoption growsUber has cut 10% of jobs in its customer service division and asked some remote staff to relocate to office hubs. The move marks the company's clearest link yet between AI adoption, organisational simplification and workforce changes.

Adani seeks rule change to launch airline, challenge IndiGo-Air India duopolyAdani Group has asked the government to relax a rule that limits airport operators' stakes in scheduled airlines. The proposal could open the door to a new carrier even as officials weigh safeguards and existing airlines raise conflict concerns.

Sensex, Nifty extend losses as Middle East tensions push oil past $95The BSE Sensex fell 314.62 points, or 0.41%, to 76,440.43, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 82.50 points, or 0.34%, to 23,913.75 around 9:30 am.

Google posts blockbuster quarter but Wall Street is worried about the billAlphabet reported a strong June quarter led by record Google Cloud growth and higher advertising revenue. Even so, investors focused on its bigger AI spending plan and the delayed Gemini rollout.

Why Tier-II and Tier-III cities are driving India's next consumption boomFor years, India's consumer story centred on the metros. Today, smaller cities are rapidly taking the lead in spending, aspirations and premium consumption. Why are Tier-II and Tier-III cities booming?

Govt rules out using unclaimed LIC, EPFO funds for other purposesThe government told the Lok Sabha there is no proposal to divert unclaimed LIC funds or EPFO inoperative account money. It said the existing transfer and settlement mechanisms will continue while outreach to claimants is stepped up.

US-India trade deal likely in 3-4 months, says senior US officialA senior US official has said that negotiations between the two countries are almost complete, with only a few procedural steps remaining before the deal can be finalised.

Middle East conflict could drag global growth to 1.3% in 2026, warns World BankAccording to World Bank Chief Economist Indermit Gill, if hostilities continue for several more months, the world could face slower growth, higher inflation and rising borrowing costs.

Sensex ends 715 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; IndiGo down 4%The BSE Sensex fell 715.06 points, or 0.92%, to close at 76,755.05, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 191.45 points, or 0.79%, to settle at 23,996.25, slipping below the key 24,000 mark.

entertainment.headline: Don't extend protest, will send food from home: Salman Khan's offer to Wangchuk Salman Khan has urged students protesting the NEET-UG paper leak to return home, assuring their safety and expressing confidence in strict action against the culprits. He also appealed to social activist Sonam Wangchuk to end his hunger strike, offering to send him home-cooked food.

Did Rajkummar Rao really comment being 'under pressure' amid students' protest? After backing students protesting the alleged NEET-UG paper leak, actor Rajkummar Rao's alleged, now-deleted reply to a comment about the 'Modi Hai Toh Mumkin Hai' song sparked fresh debate. His mention of "pressure" prompted widespread speculation, though he did not clarify what he meant.

We watched Jana Nayagan: Every political reference we saw, and didn't in Vijay film Vijay's Jana Nayagan reached theatres after months of censor changes that removed or muted TVK references. The final cut keeps its political undertones while adding markers of Vijay's rise from actor to Chief Minister.

We owe it to them: Kareena says students deserve better, backs nationwide protests Kareena Kapoor has backed students protesting across India over the NEET paper leak and education reforms. Her statement adds a prominent film voice to demands for accountability and a credible merit-based system.

Rakhi Sawant has tears, questions and message for PM over students' protest. Video Rakhi Sawant posted a tearful video backing students after the Chalo Sansad clashes in Delhi. Her intervention has intensified online focus on the NEET 2026 paper leak protest and police response.

The Apartment Job review: Ji Sung leads a crime comedy that embraces the absurdRelease Date:Sat Jul 11, 2026 Netflix's The Apartment Job follows a former gang boss targeting an apartment reserve fund through residents' politics. The series turns petty housing disputes into a sharp backdrop for crime, greed and absurd comedy.

Internet questions timing of Rang De Basanti star Madhavan's 'safe' post on protest R Madhavan backed students protesting over the NEET paper leak and urged action against those responsible. His delayed statement drew sharp online criticism, with many users calling it too late and too safe.

Kota Factory to Aspirants: OTT saw India's education crisis coming before Bollywood As students protest examination irregularities at Jantar Mantar, OTT dramas on education feel newly urgent. Their stories echo the pressure, uncertainty and exhaustion many young aspirants say they live with.

Sonu Nigam, visibly irritated, refuses all questions on student protest. Video Sonu Nigam refused to comment on the ongoing student protest during a media interaction. His response has triggered a social media debate as several film personalities back the students' demands.

Hollywood actor John Cusack voices support for students protesting NEET paper leak Hollywood actor John Cusack backed the CJP-led student protests by sharing Arundhati Roy's essay online. His post added to mounting support for students protesting against irregularities in the NEET-UG examination system.

Nation failed its children: Kamal Haasan blames broken system, backs CJP protest Kamal Haasan backed the Cockroach Janta Party protest and urged Wangchuk to end his fast. His post added to growing film industry support for students amid nationwide protest over NEET irregularities and the demand for education reforms.

Naseeruddin Shah says celebs silent on students' protest are like 'dogs with bone' Naseeruddin Shah has backed students protesting at Jantar Mantar and questioned Bollywood's silence. His remarks came as the agitation over exam irregularities and alleged police action drew wider film industry support.

For the students. By the students: Alia Bhatt lends support Alia Bhatt has voiced support for students protesting at Jantar Mantar over the NEET paper leak and education reforms. Her message adds to the voices from the film industry backing students seeking accountability and wider attention to their demands.

Video: Trisha arrives at Chennai theatre to watch Vijay's Jana Nayagan Trisha Krishnan arrived at Rohini Theatre in Chennai to watch Vijay's Jana Nayagan on release day. Her visit added to the attention around the delayed film, which is being billed as Vijay's farewell release.

A Shop for Killers S2 first impression: Lee Dong-wook is back, and so is the chaosRelease Date:Wed Jul 22, 2026 A Shop for Killers returns for its second season with Lee Dong-wook back as Jeong Jin-man and Ji-an facing new threats. The opening episodes keep the action tight and the mystery alive, even as the expanding world tests the emotional core.

Did Vijay charge Rs 220 crore for Jana Nayagan? Cast's reported fees explained Reports claim Thalapathy Vijay earned between Rs 220 crore to Rs 275 crore for Jana Nayagan, potentially making it one of Indian cinema's biggest paydays. Here's a look at the reported salaries of H Vinoth, Anirudh Ravichander, Pooja Hegde, Bobby Deol and the rest of the cast.

Jana Nayagan review: Vijay deserved a better goodbye than this bloated spectacleRelease Date:Thu Jul 23, 2026 Jana Nayagan movie review: Director H Vinoth's film, starring Vijay, Mamitha Baiju and Bobby Deol, wants to be both a commercial entertainer and a TVK manifesto, and ends up doing justice to neither.

Jana Nayagan's title card isn't a tribute to CM Vijay. It's a cheap shot at Stalin Jana Nayagan's title card gave Vijay's fans exactly what they wanted: a sitting Chief Minister as the hero. But burying an old, borrowed swipe at Stalin inside it turned a well-earned victory lap into an unnecessary, cheap parting shot.

Jana Nayagan X review: Vijay shines in an average film, say fans Jana Nayagan opened in theatres on Wednesday after a seven-month delay, with early X reviews split on Vijay's final film. The response highlights strong fan interest in his swansong even as viewers debate its story, length and second-half execution.

general news.headline: Rahul Gandhi leads INDIA bloc MPs' march at Gandhi Smriti amid NEET protestRahul Gandhi led INDIA bloc MPs towards Gandhi Smriti, but their buses were stopped outside his Delhi residence. The move was part of the Opposition's protest over the NEET paper leak and the police action against student demonstrators.

Exclusive: Pellet guns used against protesters in Delhi. Wounds, medical papers reveal truthPellet guns, mostly associated with Jammu and Kashmir, have been used in New Delhi, claim protesters. India Today Digital spoke to a protester whose medico-legal document says he was hit by pellets. He was hit during the CJP's march to Parliament when security personnel allegedly used excessive force.

Live: Mobile internet shut within 1.5 km of Jantar Mantar till midnightCJP Protest in Delhi Live Updates: Delhi remained tense as NEET paper leak protests continued, with the Centre renewing its offer for talks. Union Minister Jitendra Singh said he and Health Minister JP Nadda were ready to meet protesting students at any venue, but the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) insisted discussions be held at the protest site or a neutral location. Business groups expressed concern over heavy losses caused by metro station closures and traffic disruptions, while traders in Connaught Place were advised to shut early. Opposition leaders, including Priyanka Gandhi, criticised the police action against protesters, while BJP MP Tejaswi Surya defended the government's education reforms. A Delhi Police ACP was injured in stone-pelting, CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke briefly left the protest due to illness, and Gitanjali Angmo backed the students' peaceful agitation. Stay with Indiatoday.in for the latest updates.

Swords, religious chants: CJP's Jantar Mantar protest at risk of being hijackedCommunal slogans, Nihangs carrying weapons, unannounced marches and smaller gatherings are now defining the Cockroach Janta Party's (CJP's) protest at New Delhi's Jantar Mantar. The core issue of education now seems to have been lost in the chaos, and there are risks of diverse interests hijacking the protest.

I found out why thousands are flooding Jantar Mantar now. It's not Wangchuk or DipkeA protest once confined to a few thousand people has now swelled into a crowd too large to count accurately. Protesters say the July 20 lathi-charge and use of tear gas by Delhi Police and RAF personnel has transformed the frustration of students into anger, triggering a fresh wave of agitation at Jantar Mantar.

Sooryavanshi's maiden fifty helps Shreyas Iyer register first win as India captainZIM vs IND, 1st T20I: India's seven-match winless run in T20Is came to an end in Harare on Thursday as Mayank Yadav's hostile spell and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's record-breaking half-century sealed a commanding eight-wicket win over Zimbabwe in the series opener.

Bumper monsoon: 10,000-km-long rain cloud seen from India to South KoreaSatellite imagery has captured a vast monsoon rain band stretching from north India to South Korea. The spell has sharply lifted rainfall across India while prompting flood and landslide warnings in vulnerable regions.

Govt's 'your convenience, any time' appeal for talks. CJP has a conditionThe government again invited the protesting students for talks, saying four proposals were sent in the last 24 hours. The outreach came as the CJP called for nationwide protests on Friday.

How Akhilesh Yadav is using three stages in Delhi for one big gainFrom the CJP's Jantar Mantar platform shunned by ally Congress to Rahul Gandhi's 7 LKM protest to Parliament, Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav is using all stages in Delhi. The goal seems to be one, coming just ahead of the Assembly election in Uttar Pradesh.

CJI vows intervention if Supreme Court metro station stays shut amid CJP protestThe Chief Justice said the Supreme Court administration had already been asked to take up the matter with the Delhi Metro authorities and assured the Bar that if the issue remained unresolved by lunchtime, he would personally intervene.

Congress's protest near PM's house insincere: Big remark by Wangchuk's wifeGitanjali Angmo, in an interview with India Today's Rajdeep Sardesai, criticised Rahul Gandhi's flash protest near Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence and said leaders should have joined students at Jantar Mantar instead.

Don't extend protest, will send food from home: Salman Khan's offer to WangchukSalman Khan has urged students protesting the NEET-UG paper leak to return home, assuring their safety and expressing confidence in strict action against the culprits. He also appealed to social activist Sonam Wangchuk to end his hunger strike, offering to send him home-cooked food.

Bengaluru CEO rides scooter inside office to celebrate company's 'amazing quarter'A Bengaluru-based CEO rode a scooty through the NoBroker office while celebrating his Packers and Movers team's record quarter, before handing it to the winning employee in a now-viral video.

Teen protester says he may lose eyesight after pellet injury, cops reject claimThe family of a 19-year-old who joined the CJP protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar alleged that he suffered serious eye injuries, allegedly from pellet fire, and is at risk of losing his vision. The Delhi Police, however, denied using pellet guns against protesters.

Cabin crew member raped in office cab on way to Hyderabad airport, driver arrestedAccording to police, the incident took place in the afternoon of July 20, when the cabin crew member boarded an airline-arranged cab from Hyderabad's Shamshabad to the airport to report for her duty.

Infosys to roll out salary hikes from October, hire 20,000 freshersThe IT major said the salary hike cycle will be implemented in three parts. While the company did not share details of the percentage increase, it confirmed that the rollout will begin in October and continue into January for different employee groups.

She never sat for NEET. Her father's death brought her to the protestThousands have gathered at Delhi's Jantar Mantar over the past few days, demanding accountability after the NEET paper leak. But not everyone at the NEET protest was an aspirant. For 22-year-old Sneha Aarya, it wasn't about an entrance exam: it was about losing faith in a system she says failed her family.

Jana Nayagan review: Vijay deserved a better goodbye than this bloated spectacleRelease Date:Thu Jul 23, 2026Jana Nayagan movie review: Director H Vinoth's film, starring Vijay, Mamitha Baiju and Bobby Deol, wants to be both a commercial entertainer and a TVK manifesto, and ends up doing justice to neither.

Beyond NEET, where can PCB students go? India's entrance exam gapStudents who choose PCM or PCB after Class 10 can find later career switches severely restricted by India's entrance rules. Experts and students say the system offers too few second chances beyond JEE and NEET.

Friends called me a baddie: Mumbai woman who stopped police van to free protestersRiya Ahir, a Mumbai-based model, stood in front of a Mumbai Police van at Shivaji Park until detained CJP protesters were released. The viral moment turned the first-time protester into a face of anger over the NEET paper leak and wider education system challenges.

Hyderabad woman told parents she was 'going out' but went to Delhi for CJP protestA Hyderabad woman travelled to Delhi to join the CJP protest and documented the journey online. Her posts drew support on social media, including an offer of stay from filmmaker Vinod Kapri.

Strongest El Nino: For 50 days, global ocean temperature has remained hotGlobal sea-surface temperatures have stayed at record highs for 50 straight days as El Niño strengthens rapidly. Scientists say the trend reflects climate change and could intensify extreme weather worldwide.

Physics Wallah founder Alakh Pandey joins Jantar Mantar protestAlakh Pandey joined the CJP protest at Jantar Mantar and urged the government to speak with students. His intervention came after criticism over his silence and widened the row beyond NEET to education and recruitment.

Stop ghosting us: At CJP protest, students say internet ban has left them in limbo"They can hear our slogans, but they won't let our messages through." Inside the internet blackout that students say is making Delhi's CJP protest even harder to navigate.

sports.headline: Commonwealth Games: India's Putul Sonowal stuns 2023 world champion Ryan Bester India's lawn bowls campaign at the Glasgow Commonwealth Games got off to a flying start on Thursday as Putul Sonowal produced one of the biggest upsets of the opening day, defeating 2023 world champion Ryan Bester of Canada in a tie-break in the Men's Singles.

Sooryavanshi's maiden fifty helps Shreyas Iyer register first win as India captain ZIM vs IND, 1st T20I: India's seven-match winless run in T20Is came to an end in Harare on Thursday as Mayank Yadav's hostile spell and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's record-breaking half-century sealed a commanding eight-wicket win over Zimbabwe in the series opener.

Sooryavanshi pips Sachin to become youngest to score an international fifty Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scripted history by becoming the youngest player from a Full Member nation to score an international fifty. The 15-year-old surpassed Sachin Tendulkar's long-standing record with a sparkling knock against Zimbabwe in the T20I in Harare.

India beat Malta in Bowls women's pairs event in Glasgow CWG campaign opener India began their Glasgow Commonwealth Games campaign with a tense tie-break win over Malta in the women's pairs lawn bowls event on Thursday. Rupa Rani Tirkey (lead) and Pinki (skip) won the opening set 7-1 before Malta took the second 4-3 to force a tie-break.

WTC Final: The Oval returns as host for 2027 title clash The ICC has confirmed The Oval as the venue for the 2027 World Test Championship final in London. The decision comes as the qualification race remains open, with all nine Test nations still in contention.

IND vs ZIM: Ashok Sharma earns maiden India cap, Mayank Yadav returns after 21 months IND vs ZIM, 1st T20I Playing XIs: Ashok Sharma's impressive rise from IPL sensation to India international continued as the Rajasthan pacer made his T20I debut against Zimbabwe. India also welcomed back express quick Mayank Yadav after a 21-month injury-enforced absence.

Ashes 2027 schedule announced, Trent Bridge returns to host series opener The ECB has scheduled the men's and women's Ashes to run in parallel again in 2027. Trent Bridge and Headingley will serve as the openers for the men's and women's Ashes respectively.

Commonwealth Games 2026 Opening Ceremony: Timing, TV Channel, Live Streaming The Commonwealth Games 2026 open in Glasgow with a star-studded ceremony at the OVO Hydro. The event launches 11 days of competition and puts India in focus with Mirabai Chanu leading the contingent.

CJP Protest: Students use Norway's viral Viking Row with a twist in Mumbai The students in Mumbai, who are part of the CJP protests, decided to use the Norway football team's iconic Viking Row as a medium to voice their concerns in Mumbai.

How Lovlina Borgohain got India CWG 2026 medal before opening ceremony Lovlina Borgohain moved straight into the Commonwealth Games 2026 women's 75kg semifinals after a bye. That put India on the medals table before the opening ceremony, with at least bronze already assured.

Indian judoka Arun Kumar cut from CWG 2026 after doping test failureIndia judoka Arun Kumar has been ruled out of the Commonwealth Games after an adverse analytical finding in an out-of-competition test. The late notice leaves India without a replacement in the men's 73kg event and strips a medal hope on the eve of competition.

Keep faith in government: Dhawan urges patience from students amid protestsShikhar Dhawan asked protesting students to stay patient and trust the government amid the CJP protests in Delhi. The statement comes after the demonstrations over the alleged NEET-UG 2026 paper leak and examination irregularities have continued to garner nationwide attention.

Argentina fans launch petition to replay the World Cup 2026 final. Here's whyArgentina supporters have launched a petition asking FIFA to replay the 2026 World Cup final after the loss to Spain. The campaign attacks the officiating of referee Slavko Vincic, calling it biased.

Olympic gold medallist Abhinav Bindra backs students amid protestsOlympic gold medalist Abhinav Bindra shared a message on the ongoing student protests at Delhi's Jantar Mantar, urging India to strengthen its education system. His remarks come as nationwide demonstrations over the alleged NEET-UG paper leak continue to intensify.

Ramos takes a jibe at Messi by recreating Argentina star's iconic World Cup picAfter Spain beat Argentina in the 2026 World Cup final, Sergio Ramos recreated Lionel Messi's viral trophy-sleeping photo to take a light-hearted jibe at the Argentina captain. Messi and Co. were beaten by Spin 1-0 in the showdown in New York.

Rodri set for surgery after Spain's World Cup win as Real Madrid links persistSpain's World Cup-winning captain Rodri is set to undergo back surgery after a stellar tournament that earned him the Golden Ball. The latest injury setback comes as uncertainty continues over his Manchester City future, with contract talks unresolved and Real Madrid links refusing to fade.

Messi's Inter Miami faces MLS investigation over alleged Casemiro deal tamperingInter Miami's signing of Casemiro has quickly come under scrutiny after MLS launched a tampering investigation into the transfer. The probe centres on discovery rights, reviving questions over the club's transfer dealings despite securing another marquee signing alongside Lionel Messi.

Don't expect medal rush: Why India could be in for a quiet CWG 2026India has been amongst the medal when it comes to the Commonwealth Games in recent times, but this time the excitement has been watered down. A reduced roster of events and with some historic medal-winning events missing, India could be in for a quiet outing in Glasgow this year.

CWG 2026: India's full schedule, list of athletes, complete event listIndia's Commonwealth Games 2026 campaign begins on July 23 with a 126-member contingent aiming to return with around 30 medals in Glasgow. Here's the complete date-wise schedule featuring Neeraj Chopra, Mirabai Chanu, Lovlina Borgohain, Murali Sreeshankar, Tejaswin Shankar and every major Indian medal contender.

Heartbreak for Sindhu as she loses China Open pre-quarters marathon to YufeiPV Sindhu's unbeaten run has come to an end after she was beaten by World No.4 Chen Yufei in a titanic battle at the China Open on Thursday, July 23.

Shreyas Iyer's desperate India seek T20I course correction in ZimbabweZimbabwe vs India: Shreyas Iyer will be eager for his first win as India captain as his young side seeks T20I course correction against a spirited Zimbabwe in the three-match series starting in Harare on Thursday.

Who are India's top medal contenders in Commonwealth Games?India have sent a 126-member strong squad for the Commonwealth Games 2026 in Glasgow. Who are India's favourites to medal in the competition? Let's have a look.

When Novak Djokovic backed Serbia's student protest movementAs student protests gather pace in New Delhi, Novak Djokovic's support for Serbia's student movement has come back into focus. The 24-time Grand Slam champion has stood by Serbian students since nationwide protests began after the deadly Novi Sad railway station tragedy in November 2024.

Alireza Firouzja clinches Chennai Grand Masters title after draw vs ArjunFrench Grandmaster Alireza Firouzja won the fourth edition of the Chennai Grand Masters after drawing with India's Arjun Erigaisi in the seventh and final round on Wednesday.

Shreyas Iyer wants fearless India to bounce back in Zimbabwe after rough UK tourIndia captain Shreyas Iyer wants his players to play fearless cricket and ignore outside criticism after India's disappointing tour of England. He believes the difficult phase has brought the squad closer ahead of the three-match T20I series against Zimbabwe.

Rahmat Shah to lead Afghanistan in Tests and ODIs, Gurbaz becomes vice-captainThe Afghanistan Cricket Board (ACB) has appointed experienced batter Rahmat Shah as the new captain of its Test and One-Day International (ODI) teams following the resignation of Hashmatullah Shahidi.

IPL founder Lalit Modi set to return to India after 16 yearsIPL founder and former chairman Lalit Modi has said he will return to India after 16 years following a tribunal verdict that gave him major relief in the 2009 IPL South Africa Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) case.

Sindhu wildly celebrates win vs Unnati Hooda, shares cryptic social media postChina Open 2026: A huge roar on court, followed by a cryptic message off it. PV Sindhu battled past Unnati Hooda in three games before sharing a social media post that sparked plenty of intrigue.

UTT: Kolkata edge U Mumba in thriller as both teams seal semis spotKolkata Thunder Blades ended defending champions U Mumba TT's winning streak with a dramatic 8-7 victory to secure a place in the UTT Season 7 semi-finals, with both teams advancing to the knockout stage.

Benched in England, Kuldeep Yadav signs for Yorkshire for one-dayers and CountyKuldeep Yadav has joined Yorkshire after being left out of India's ODI XI in England. The stint gives him immediate match time before the Sri Lanka Tests and a county run with the English club later in the year.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
